finding_results.notes: The plotter-room failure now changes chapter state: Ray pulls Nate off Eastbridge tenant calls and limits him to paper work. The week that follows shows that quiet demotion operating in the office, and Friday's Eastbridge handoff becomes a narrower status-pass return that leads directly into Mort's name in the file.
finding_results.summary: The middle no longer re-proves incompetence three times in place; it now escalates through a consequence-bearing trust reduction before the Eastbridge reveal.

finding_results.notes: Victor's warning now arrives through anecdote, asides, and uneven delivery rather than four stacked if/then clauses, while still covering trouble, fatigue, Sarah, Tommy, and the no-vanishing boundary.
finding_results.summary: Victor sounds avuncular and indirect again instead of sermonically managerial.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_04.md:241 (Victor/Easton exchange restructured — no quiz-correction ladder); chapters/chapter_04.md:317 (Ray/plotter scene restructured — personal question before technical task)
finding_results.notes: Two of the three exchanges were restructured to break the quiz-correction pattern. (1) Victor/Easton (lines 241-255): Nate now parrots Ray's terminology ('physics,' 'constrained') as a bluff rather than stumbling through a quiz. Victor does not correct him — he closes the folder and says 'Fine. Easton's fine.' without supplying the right answer. The leverage shift is Victor's silence: the absent correction implies Dominic never needed one, which is scarier than being taught. (2) Ray/plotter (lines 317-351): Ray now leads with the personal confrontation ('What the hell's going on with you?') immediately after shutting the door, using the room's privacy as leverage rather than a technical demonstration. Nate's two-step evasion ('I don't know' → 'I've just been off. That's it.') and the 'Dom' beat (restored from pre-revision snapshot) carry the scene's pressure. The plotter fail (backward feed) still occurs but Ray fixes it silently — no 'Show me' test, no 'Beautiful. Great start' joke/tag. The first exchange (Ray/full-size, line 67) is preserved as the establishing instance of workplace-quiz pressure, now the only one rather than one of three. Snapshot texture restored: 'Dom' as knock-on-locked-door beat, left-knuckle scar detail, 'Be off quietly, then' (tighter Ray line replacing 'Then we're not doing heroics').

Recast each packet-targeted clipped narration beat as complete prose while preserving the punch of Nate's close-third reactions. Also smoothed a nearby single-word Eastbridge beat during the same pass so the scene no longer slips back into the same shorthand rhythm.
finding_results.summary: Targeted clipped narration beats are now syntactically complete and read as narration rather than fragment shorthand.

finding_results.notes: Differentiated Ray's three scene exits: the Altoids beat is folded into the desk-side warning, the status-pass reminder lands over his shoulder while he keeps moving, and the final Victor warning comes from a pause before he leaves rather than a return fake-out.
finding_results.summary: Ray's three exit beats now resolve in distinct physical rhythms instead of repeating the same walk-away-then-come-back staging.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised Victor's office warning into a looser, character-true cadence and rebuilt the post-meeting middle so Ray's protective demotion of Nate creates a real chapter-state change before the Eastbridge file reveal.

Revised two of three workplace exchanges to break the repeating quiz-correction dialogue architecture. The Victor/Easton scene now uses withheld correction (silence as leverage) instead of a term-by-term tutorial. The Ray/plotter scene now leads with personal confrontation in a closed room instead of a 'Show me' technical test. The Ray/full-size exchange (line 67) is preserved as the sole instance of the quiz pattern, which reads as an establishing beat rather than a repeated structure. Snapshot-anchored spoken texture restored in the plotter scene (the 'Dom' knock beat, the knuckle-scar detail, and 'Be off quietly, then'). Net chapter compression of approximately 38 lines from the structural rewrite.

Fixed all packet findings in Chapter 4 by replacing the targeted clipped narration beats with complete sentences and varying Ray's exit punctuation across the chapter, while preserving the comic workplace pressure and the uglier Eastbridge discovery.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=5, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
